comments.author: @stupor_mundi
comments.text: The Spanish music in the background and the Spanish pronunciation of Portuguese words (particularly words with 'J') is very disappointing. Portugal and Spain are culturally very different, in fact they were historical enemies for many centuries, so hearing Spanish music in the background and Spanish pronounciation in a video about Portugal is like nails on a chalkboard. It wouldn't have taken that much more work to doublecheck the pronounciation or at least use music that is not so obviously native to Spain like at 9:35.

comments.author: @Module_79L
comments.text: 1:16 - Where does these AI slop machines get their data from? Portugal doesn't have 1,700 kms of coast line, the mainland has around 943 kms and if we include the Azores and Madeira archipelagos, it has around 2,500 kms. Even Wikipedia, which sometimes is not the best source for accurate information, says 943!

comments.author: @ivonerei8424
comments.text:
Congratulations on the video. Just one small note: the river that flows through Lisbon is the Rio Tejo, or Tejo River, rather than the Tagus River. Since you used Portuguese words such as azulejos and francesinha, it would make sense to also keep the original name of the river.

Of course, there would be much more to say about Portugal, especially about our food, traditions and culture, but overall it is a good video about the country. Thank you.

I would be happy to help with any future videos about Portugal.
